{{Quote|Say, old flatfoot, this isn't the way to Teguta Lusat!|Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCMS" />}}
'''Teguta Lusat''' was a [[City/Legends|city]] on [[Rafa IV]] that served as the colonial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa system]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. Located in the shadows of the massive, world-spanning ruins of the vanished [[Sharu/Legends|Sharu]] civilization, Teguta Lusat was badly damaged during the "Sharu Awakening" of [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], when the activation of the [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]] by colonial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] [[Duttes Mer]] caused the Sharu ruins to open and reveal vast stretches of ancient cityscape. Although most of the city's [[Human/Legends|Human]] residents fled after the return of the Sharu, Teguta Lusat remained as a center for those that remained.

==Description==
One of the only major settlements on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rafa IV]] in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]], Teguta Lusat was a small, primarily [[Human/Legends|Human]] town awkwardly wedged in between the massive, world-spanning ruins of the ancient [[Sharu/Legends|Sharu]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The Sharu buildings were some of the largest artificial structures known in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]—in the little space that remained, Teguta Lusat was formed out of narrow, oddly angled boardwalked streets and Human-scale dwellings and businesses built up against titanic Sharu walls. As the center of a thriving colony, Teguta Lusat was relatively bustling, with many [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] and bars, and had a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] located a short [[Speeder bus/Legends|hoverbus]] ride from city center. Although predominantly Human, Teguta Lusat had a large population of the Rafa system's native [[Toka]], who were treated as little more than domestic animals by many of the settlers.<ref name="LCMS" />

==History==
{{Quote|It was terrifying to the savage that I was, for example, to brush near an ancient wall, as I did that night in Teguta Lusat, and appear an instant later, leagues away, amidst a gathering of my people.|Mohs to Lando Calrissian|<ref name="LCMS" />}}
By [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]], Teguta Lusat held the status of the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] settlement of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-run colony in the Rafa system [[Governor/Legends|governed]] by [[Duttes Mer]]. In [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]], Mer—working along with [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]]—lured [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] to Teguta Lusat to force him to track down the [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]], a powerful Sharu artifact. Along with [[Vuffi Raa]], a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] of obscure origin programmed to serve Mer, Calrissian departed for [[Rafa V]] to search for the Mindharp and subsequently disappeared for four months, before suddenly reappearing out of nowhere in the streets of Teguta Lusat, having been instantly transported back there after retrieving the artifact.<ref name="LCMS" />

Mer had Calrissian imprisoned and took the Mindharp for himself, believing that playing the "Ultimate Instrument of Music" would allow him to take control of the minds of all within the system. However, he found instead that the Mindharp was the key to restoring the Sharu civilization, by reverting the primitive Toka back to their highly intelligent former forms. Mer was killed upon activating the Mindharp,<ref name="LCMS" /> and Teguta Lusat itself was heavily damaged as the massive Sharu structures surrounding it opened to reveal kilometer upon kilometer of highly advanced cityscape. Most Human colonists elected to leave after the "Sharu Awakening," but the majority that stayed—mainly researchers, scientists and the poor—stayed in the remnants of Teguta Lusat, reduced greatly from its former self.<ref name="gamer5">{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Teguta Lusat appeared in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', a novel written by [[L. Neil Smith]] and released in [[1983]].<ref name="LCMS" />
